title: Rapid double-submit of login form creates duplicate concurrent authentication requests — race condition producing two sessions
description: Rapidly double-clicking (or keyboard-hammering) the submit button before the server responds can dispatch two simultaneous POST /auth/login requests. If the server or client does not debounce/idempotency-guard, this can create duplicate sessions, duplicate audit log entries, or trigger account-lockout thresholds prematurely on a good-credential login.
expected_result: PASS: Only one authentication request is sent to the server (confirmed via network tab); only one session token is issued; the submit button is disabled after the first click until the response arrives. FAIL: Two identical POST requests are dispatched — this can cause duplicate session records, race conditions in account-lockout counters (e.g. incrementing failed-attempt counters twice for one attempt), or the second request arriving after a logout that invalidates the first token, causing a confusing UX loop.

test_steps.action: Fill in a valid email address
Click the submit button a first time
test_steps.expected: First click dispatches the login request; button may show a loading state

test_steps.action: Immediately click the submit button a second time without waiting for the response
test_steps.expected: Second click is either ignored (button is disabled/loading) or dispatches a second request — capture which behaviour occurs

test_steps.action: Wait for the page to settle after both clicks
test_steps.expected: Exactly one authenticated session is established; user is redirected once to the dashboard

test_steps.action: Verify the submit button was disabled or showed a loading indicator after the first click to prevent double submission
test_steps.expected: Submit button is in a disabled or loading state between click 1 and server response, preventing duplicate requests
